family_profile.observance: Reform/Conservative, observes Shabbat and major holidays

communications.subject: Important: Mid-Winter Break Schedule Reminder
communications.body:
Dear Seattle Public Schools Families,

This is a reminder that Mid-Winter Break is scheduled for February 17-21, 2026. All schools will be closed. Extended day programs will also be closed during this time.

School resumes on Monday, February 24, 2026.

Best regards,
Seattle Public Schools Communications

communications.subject: Green Lake Elementary Weekly Newsletter - February 10
communications.body:
Dear Green Lake Families,

Welcome to a new week! Here are this week's highlights:

• Parent-Teacher Conferences: Sign-ups are now open on our school website. Conferences will be held February 18-19 during Mid-Winter Break.
• Science Fair: Projects are due March 3. Students should be working on their experiments at home.
• Lost and Found: Please check the cafeteria for missing items. Unclaimed items will be donated on February 28.
• Valentine's Day: Celebration on Friday, February 14. Students may bring valentines for classmates.

Have a great week!
Dr. Jennifer Martinez, Principal
Green Lake Elementary

communications.subject: 3rd Grade: This Week's Learning Goals
communications.body:
Good morning 3rd grade families!

This week in class:

READING: We're starting a new chapter book, 'Charlotte's Web.' Please encourage your child to read 20 minutes each evening.

MATH: Multiplication facts (6s, 7s, 8s). Practice at home with flashcards or online games.

WRITING: Persuasive essay rough drafts due Thursday, February 13.

SCIENCE: Plant life cycles - we'll be planting seeds on Wednesday!

Reminder: Valentine's Day party is Friday. Students may bring valentines for classmates (24 total). No food items please due to allergies.

Best,
Ms. Rachel Thompson
3rd Grade Teacher

communications.subject: URGENT: Maya Cohen IEP Annual Review Meeting - February 18
communications.body:
Dear Mr. and Mrs. Cohen,

This email serves as official notice of Maya Cohen's Annual IEP Review Meeting.

DATE: Tuesday, February 18, 2026
TIME: 10:00 AM - 11:30 AM
LOCATION: Green Lake Elementary, Conference Room B

ATTENDEES:
- Parents (Sarah and David Cohen)
- Special Education Coordinator (Linda Patel)
- General Education Teacher (Rachel Thompson)
- Resource Teacher (James Kim)
- School Psychologist (Dr. Emily Warren)

We will review Maya's progress on her current IEP goals, discuss assessment results, and develop goals for the upcoming year. Please review the attached draft IEP prior to the meeting.

If you cannot attend, please contact me immediately at (206) 555-0198 to reschedule. Both parents' participation is strongly encouraged.

Sincerely,
Linda Patel, M.Ed.
Special Education Coordinator
Green Lake Elementary

communications.subject: Class of 2026: Important Graduation Deadlines
communications.body:
Dear Senior Families,

As we approach the second semester, here are critical deadlines for graduation:

• February 28: Senior portraits due for yearbook
• March 15: Cap and gown orders (Jostens)
• April 1: Grad Night permission slips and payment ($75)
• May 1: Senior awards nomination forms
• June 10: Graduation ceremony (Memorial Stadium, 7:00 PM)

REMINDER: All seniors must have completed 24 credits and passed the state assessment to graduate. Check Skyward to verify your student's credit status.

College-bound students: Don't forget to send final transcripts to your chosen university after acceptance.

Go Roughriders!
Laura Bennett
Senior Class Advisor

communications.subject: This Week's Basketball Schedule + Important Team Dinner Info
communications.body:
Roughrider Basketball Families,

Here's this week's schedule:

MONDAY 2/10: Practice 3:30-5:30 PM (gym)
WEDNESDAY 2/12: HOME GAME vs. Garfield High, 7:00 PM tip-off
  - Senior Night recognition at 6:45 PM
  - Parents please arrive by 6:30 PM for photos
FRIDAY 2/14: Practice 3:30-5:00 PM (gym)

TEAM DINNER: Wednesday before the game, 5:00 PM at Pagliacci Pizza (University Village). Please RSVP to team mom Jennifer by Tuesday.

SENIOR NIGHT: This is a big deal for our seniors! We'll be recognizing Josh Cohen, Marcus Williams, and Tyler Anderson. Parents, please bring flowers and be ready for photos on the court.

Let's pack the gym and support our Roughriders!

Coach Marcus Johnson
Varsity Basketball

communications.message: Hi everyone! Quick reminder that Valentine's Day is this Friday. Ms. Thompson said no food items due to allergies. Stickers, pencils, or small toys are great alternatives! 💝
Parents! Senior Night for basketball is Wednesday. If your child is being recognized, please arrive by 6:30 PM for photos. Bring flowers! 🏀🌹

communications.subject: Schedule Update: Shift Swap Request
communications.body:
Hi Sarah,

Jenna Martinez is requesting to swap her Thursday 2/13 day shift (7am-7pm) for your Friday 2/14 evening shift (7pm-7am). Are you available to make this swap?

Please confirm by end of day Tuesday so I can update the schedule.

Thanks,
Linda Thompson
Nurse Manager, Med-Surg Unit
Swedish Medical Center

communications.subject: Shabbat Shalom - This Week at Beth Shalom
communications.body:
Dear Friends,

Shabbat Shalom! Join us this week:

FRIDAY EVENING:
6:30 PM - Kabbalat Shabbat service
7:30 PM - Oneg Shabbat (dessert reception)

SATURDAY MORNING:
10:00 AM - Shabbat morning service
11:30 AM - Kiddush lunch

This week's Torah portion is Mishpatim (Exodus 21:1-24:18), which deals with laws of justice and compassion.

SUNDAY:
Hebrew School resumes 9:30 AM - 12:00 PM

Warm regards,
Rabbi Sarah Goldstein
Congregation Beth Shalom

communications.title: Maya Cohen - Annual IEP Review Meeting
communications.location: Green Lake Elementary, Conference Room B
communications.description: Annual IEP review for Maya. Attendees: Parents, Special Ed Coordinator, Teachers, School Psychologist. Review progress and set new goals for the upcoming year.

communications.group_name: Green Lake Soccer U9 Team Parents
communications.message: Good morning! Practice today at 5:30 PM at Green Lake Park (lower field). Please bring water bottles and shin guards. We'll be working on passing drills. ⚽

communications.subject: Volunteers Needed: Science Fair Setup (March 2)
communications.body:
Dear PTA Members,

Our annual Science Fair is coming up on March 3, and we need volunteers to help with setup on March 2 (evening, 6-8 PM).

Tasks include:
- Setting up display tables in the gym
- Hanging banners and decorations
- Organizing judging materials

Please sign up here: [SignUpGenius link]

Thank you for supporting our students!

Jennifer Park
PTA President, Green Lake Elementary

communications.subject: Winter Concert Reminder - February 20 + Dress Rehearsal
communications.body:
Dear Band Families,

Our Winter Concert is coming up!

DRESS REHEARSAL: Wednesday, February 19, 3:30-5:30 PM (mandatory)
CONCERT: Thursday, February 20, 7:00 PM (Roosevelt Auditorium)

DRESS CODE:
- Black pants or skirt
- White dress shirt
- Black dress shoes
- Band t-shirt will be provided

Students should arrive by 6:30 PM on concert night. Doors open to audience at 6:45 PM.

Tickets are not required, but donations are appreciated. Suggested donation: $5 per person.

Looking forward to a great performance!

Thomas Anderson
Band Director, Roosevelt High School

communications.subject: Josh's Lesson Today - 4:30 PM
communications.body:
Hi David,

Just confirming Josh's trumpet lesson today at 4:30 PM at my studio (Benaroya Hall, Room 305).

This week we'll be working on the solo piece for his spring recital - the Haydn Trumpet Concerto. Please make sure Josh brings his mute and the sheet music.

See you this afternoon!

Dr. Elena Martinez
Seattle Symphony, Principal Trumpet

communications.message: Hi everyone! Please RSVP for tomorrow's team dinner at Pagliacci (5 PM). I need a headcount by tonight. Reply here or text me directly. Thanks! 🍕

communications.subject: Travel Approval: Everett Site Visit - February 26-27
communications.body:
David,

Your travel request for the Everett facility visit has been approved.

DATES: February 26-27, 2026
PURPOSE: Operations review and Q1 planning meeting
HOTEL: Courtyard Marriott Everett (booked)
RENTAL CAR: Enterprise (confirmation sent separately)

Please submit your expense report within 5 business days of return.

Let me know if you need anything else.

Tom Harrison
Director, Operations
Boeing Commercial Airplanes

communications.subject: Hebrew School This Sunday - Purim Planning!
communications.body:
Dear Hebrew School Families,

This Sunday, February 16, we'll be preparing for Purim! Classes will focus on the Megillah story and students will start working on costumes.

Purim Carnival: Sunday, March 9, 2:00-4:00 PM
- Costume contest
- Games and activities
- Hamantaschen baking
- Megillah reading

Please mark your calendars! More details to come.

L'Shalom,
Rachel Levine
Hebrew School Principal
